findings.title: aria-selected on buttons without role="tablist" parent in Nav mode
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users — aria-selected is not valid on plain buttons
findings.description: Six nav-tab buttons use aria-selected but their container (#navTabs) has no role="tablist" in the default Nav mode. Sections use role="tabpanel" without a tablist parent. axe-core confirms aria-allowed-attr violation on 6 nodes. Tablist mode (toggle) works correctly.
findings.fix: In Nav mode: replace aria-selected with aria-current="page" on the active tab. Remove role="tabpanel" from sections. Only apply tablist/tab/tabpanel roles when Tablist mode is enabled.
findings.legal_exposure: Level A violation. All 4 jurisdictions require Level A compliance.

findings.title: 8 elements below 4.5:1 contrast ratio
findings.affected_users: Low-vision users, elderly users, users in bright environments
findings.description: axe-core identified 8 elements with insufficient contrast. Likely affected: text using --text-light (#566573) on certain backgrounds, muted helper text, and tooltip content.
findings.fix: Check each flagged element. For --text-light on white background: current #566573 gives 5.9:1 (passes). Failures may be on colored card backgrounds or in dark mode. Verify each combination.
findings.legal_exposure: Level AA. Top-3 most litigated criterion in US ADA lawsuits.

findings.title: 3 elements outside landmark regions
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users navigating by landmarks
findings.location: Theme toggle button, chat trigger button, SVG filters
findings.description: axe-core region rule: 3 floating elements (theme toggle, chat trigger, SVG defs) are outside any landmark. The SVG is aria-hidden so not impactful. The two buttons are fixed-position UI that could be wrapped in a complementary landmark.
findings.fix: Wrap theme toggle and chat trigger in a <div role="complementary" aria-label="Page tools">.

findings.title: No Schema.org JSON-LD structured data
findings.affected_users: AI agents, search engines, answer engines
findings.description: No <script type="application/ld+json"> found. Adding Article or WebPage schema would improve discoverability by AI answer engines.
findings.fix: Add JSON-LD with @type: WebPage or Article, including name, description, author, dateModified.

findings.title: No canonical URL
findings.description: No <link rel="canonical"> found. The og:url is set correctly but canonical link is missing.

findings.title: Navigation container uses <div> instead of <nav>
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users navigating by landmarks
findings.description: The main navigation uses <div id="site-nav" aria-label="學習旅程導航"> instead of <nav>. While aria-label provides a name, <nav> is the semantic element for navigation.
findings.fix: Change <div id="site-nav" class="nav-container" aria-label="學習旅程導航"> to <nav id="site-nav" class="nav-container" aria-label="學習旅程導航">.

jurisdictions.name: Taiwan
jurisdictions.law: 身心障礙者權益保障法
jurisdictions.detail: 0 critical violations. Educational site with strong a11y foundations.

jurisdictions.name: Japan JIS
jurisdictions.law: 障害者差別解消法 + JIS X 8341-3
jurisdictions.detail: 0 critical. ARIA mismatch is the only Level A issue, mitigated by Tablist mode toggle.

jurisdictions.name: US ADA
jurisdictions.law: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III
jurisdictions.detail: 0 critical. 8 contrast issues flagged by axe. Educational/nonprofit likely lower litigation target.

jurisdictions.name: EU EAA
jurisdictions.detail: 0 critical. In force since Jun 2025. ARIA and contrast fixes recommended before EU deployment.

remediation.title: Fix Nav mode ARIA: aria-selected -> aria-current, remove role=tabpanel
Investigate and fix 8 contrast failures flagged by axe-core
Change #site-nav from div to nav element

testing_recommendations: Run axe-core in CI (GitHub Actions) on every push
Keyboard-only test monthly (Tab through all 6 sections)
Screen reader test per release (VoiceOver + NVDA)
Check contrast in both light and dark mode after CSS changes
